code_snippets.caption: Clinical data validation query
code_snippets.code:
WITH patient_metrics AS (
  SELECT 
    patient_id,
    test_date,
    test_result,
    CASE 
      WHEN test_result IS NULL THEN 'Missing'
      WHEN test_result < 0 THEN 'Invalid'
      ELSE 'Valid'
    END as data_quality
  FROM clinical_tests
  WHERE test_date >= DATEADD(month, -3, GETDATE())
)
SELECT 
  data_quality,
  COUNT(*) as record_count,
  ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) as percentage
FROM patient_metrics
GROUP BY data_quality;

results: Processed 1M+ clinical records daily with 99% accuracy
Reduced report generation time by 75% through automation
Enabled data-driven decisions for 50+ healthcare professionals
Improved operational efficiency through real-time dashboards
lessons: Healthcare data requires strict validation and compliance measures
User-friendly visualizations are crucial for clinical adoption
Cross-functional collaboration enhances insight quality

title: Real-time Analytics Platform
summary: Built streaming analytics platform for Qualcomm Technologies Inc processing high-volume data with real-time insights and monitoring.
problem: Business teams needed real-time insights from high-volume data streams to make immediate operational and strategic decisions.
solution_overview: Implemented streaming analytics architecture with Kafka for ingestion, Spark for processing, and BigQuery for analytical queries with comprehensive monitoring.

architecture.alt: Real-time analytics platform architecture showing streaming data flow

my_contributions: Designed streaming data architecture for high-throughput processing
Built real-time data processing pipelines using Spark Streaming
Implemented comprehensive monitoring and alerting systems
Optimized system performance for cost-effective operations

Ensuring system reliability and fault tolerance

code_snippets.caption: Real-time stream processing with Spark
code_snippets.code:
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.types import *

def process_realtime_stream(spark):
    # Read from Kafka stream
    df = spark \
        .readStream \
        .format("kafka") \
        .option("kafka.bootstrap.servers", "localhost:9092") \
        .option("subscribe", "events") \
        .load()
    
    # Parse and transform data
    parsed_df = df.select(
        col("timestamp").cast("timestamp"),
        from_json(col("value").cast("string"), event_schema).alias("data")
    ).select(
        col("timestamp"),
        col("data.event_type"),
        col("data.user_id"),
        col("data.metrics")
    )
    
    # Aggregate in real-time
    aggregated = parsed_df \
        .groupBy(
            window(col("timestamp"), "1 minute"),
            col("event_type")
        ) \
        .agg(
            count("*").alias("event_count"),
            avg("metrics.value").alias("avg_value")
        )
    
    return aggregated

results: Processing 500K+ events per second with 99.9% reliability
Achieved sub-second query response times for real-time analytics
Reduced infrastructure costs by 40% through optimization
Enabled real-time business intelligence and decision making
lessons: Real-time systems require careful architecture planning for scalability
Monitoring and alerting are critical for production streaming systems
Performance optimization can significantly reduce operational costs

title: GCP Streaming Analytics Pipeline
summary: Production-grade streaming data pipeline on Google Cloud Platform for real-time event processing with windowing and late data handling.
problem: High-volume event streams require real-time processing for immediate insights, fraud detection, and operational monitoring with handling of out-of-order and late-arriving data.
solution_overview: Built streaming pipeline using Pub/Sub for ingestion, Apache Beam/Dataflow for processing with windowing strategies, and BigQuery for analytics with partitioning and clustering.

architecture.alt: GCP Streaming Analytics Pipeline architecture showing real-time processing

my_contributions: Designed streaming architecture with windowing strategies (tumbling, sliding, session)
Implemented late data handling with watermarks and allowed lateness
Built stateful processing for user session tracking
Optimized BigQuery with partitioning and clustering for cost efficiency
Created Dagster orchestration for pipeline monitoring
challenges: Handling high-volume streams with minimal latency
Processing late-arriving and out-of-order events
Optimizing costs while maintaining performance
Managing stateful processing across distributed systems

code_snippets.caption: Windowing with late data handling
code_snippets.code:
def process_streaming_events(pipeline):
    events = (pipeline
        | 'ReadFromPubSub' >> beam.io.ReadFromPubSub(topic=TOPIC)
        | 'ParseJSON' >> beam.Map(parse_event)
        | 'AddTimestamp' >> beam.Map(add_event_time)
        | 'Window' >> beam.WindowInto(
            beam.window.FixedWindows(60),  # 1-minute windows
            allowed_lateness=3600,  # 1 hour late data
            accumulation_mode=beam.AccumulationMode.ACCUMULATING
        )
        | 'Aggregate' >> beam.CombinePerKey(aggregate_events)
    )
    return events

results: Processed millions of events per hour with sub-minute latency
Successfully handled late-arriving data up to 1 hour after window close
Reduced BigQuery costs by 40% through strategic partitioning and clustering
Enabled real-time analytics for business intelligence and decision making
lessons: Windowing strategies are critical for streaming analytics
Late data handling requires careful watermark management
Cost optimization in cloud services requires understanding of pricing models
Stateful processing enables complex analytics but adds complexity

title: Databricks Lakehouse Platform
summary: Production-grade lakehouse architecture on Databricks with Delta Lake, combining data lakes and warehouses with ACID transactions.
problem: Modern data platforms need to handle both batch and streaming data while maintaining data quality, ACID guarantees, and high performance in a unified platform.
solution_overview: Built lakehouse architecture using Delta Lake with Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion pattern, supporting both batch and streaming processing with ACID transactions and performance optimization.

architecture.alt: Databricks Lakehouse Platform architecture showing medallion pattern

my_contributions: Designed medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold) for data quality progression
Implemented Delta Lake for ACID transactions and time travel capabilities

Optimized performance with Z-ordering, OPTIMIZE, and VACUUM operations
Created Databricks jobs for batch and streaming orchestration
challenges: Unifying batch and streaming processing in single platform
Optimizing Delta Lake performance for large datasets
Managing schema evolution across Bronze/Silver/Gold layers
Balancing ACID guarantees with performance

code_snippets.caption: Delta Lake Bronze to Silver transformation
code_snippets.code:
def bronze_to_silver(bronze_df):
    """Transform Bronze (raw) to Silver (cleaned) layer."""
    silver_df = (bronze_df
        .filter(col('quality_score') > 0.95)  # Data quality filter
        .dropDuplicates(['id', 'timestamp'])  # Deduplication
        .withColumn('enriched_data', enrich_with_reference(col('id')))
        .select('id', 'timestamp', 'event_type', 'enriched_data')
    )
    
    # Write to Delta Lake Silver with merge
    silver_df.write.format('delta').mode('overwrite').save(SILVER_PATH)
    
    # Optimize for query performance
    spark.sql(f"OPTIMIZE delta.`{SILVER_PATH}` ZORDER BY (id, timestamp)")
    
    return silver_df

results: Unified batch and streaming processing in single lakehouse platform
Enabled ACID transactions and time travel for data reliability
Optimized query performance by 60% through Z-ordering and compaction
Implemented medallion architecture ensuring data quality progression
lessons: Lakehouse architecture combines best of data lakes and warehouses
Delta Lake provides ACID guarantees without sacrificing performance
Medallion pattern ensures data quality progression
Performance optimization is crucial for large-scale analytics

code_snippets.caption: Great Expectations data quality check
code_snippets.code:
def validate_data_quality(df, expectation_suite):
    """Validate data quality using Great Expectations."""
    ge_df = ge.from_pandas(df)
    
    # Run expectation suite
    results = ge_df.validate(
        expectation_suite=expectation_suite,
        result_format='SUMMARY'
    )
    
    # Check results and alert on failures
    if not results['success']:
        failed_expectations = [
            exp for exp in results['results'] 
            if not exp['success']
        ]
        
        alert_manager.send_alert(
            level='critical',
            message=f"Data quality check failed: {len(failed_expectations)} violations",
            details=failed_expectations
        )
    
    return results

results: Achieved 99%+ data quality through automated validation
Enabled complete data lineage tracking for compliance and debugging
Maintained 95%+ SLA compliance through proactive monitoring
Detected anomalies in real-time reducing data issues by 80%
lessons: Data observability is critical for production data platforms
Automated quality checks catch issues before they impact downstream
Lineage tracking is essential for debugging and compliance
SLA monitoring ensures data reliability and trust

cover_image.alt: Data Observability Platform showing quality metrics and monitoring
